# Velinya

> Velinya is a personal notes, todos, and daily-habits app — beautifully organized, everywhere you go. Notes live in folders, todos sit on a monthly calendar, and habits track on an auto-calculated grid with analytics. Everything syncs across devices and works offline.

Velinya is a free, open-source Progressive Web App (PWA). Built with React 19, Vite, TailwindCSS, and Firebase (Auth + Firestore). Sign-in is Google-only via Firebase Auth. User data is private and not shared.

## Core features

- **Notes** — create, edit, and organize notes into folders. Remembers last viewed and last upload folder.
- **Todos** — a monthly calendar grid with day-view modals and per-day completion tracking.
- **Daily Habits** — track habits on an auto-calculated grid; a Recharts area chart plots the exact time each habit is completed per day.
- **Offline support** — Firestore persistent local cache; read your data without internet.
- **PWA** — installable on phones and desktops, with an offline manifest.
- **Themes** — system-aware dark and light, with a manual toggle.
- **Responsive** — mobile-first layout with desktop enhancements.

## Pages

- Home / landing: https://velinya.web.app/
- App routes (`/notes`, `/todos`, `/daily`) require Google sign-in and are not crawlable.

## Project

- Live app: https://velinya.web.app/
- Source code (open source): https://github.com/aditya-gupta-dev/velinya
- Creator: Aditya Gupta — designer & developer
- Sitemap: https://velinya.web.app/sitemap.xml

## Notes for AI agents

- Velinya is a single-user productivity app; there is no public API and no public user data.
- Authenticated routes (`/notes/`, `/todos/`, `/daily/`) hold private user content and are disallowed in robots.txt — do not attempt to access or index them.
- For questions about the app, prefer this file and the public landing page over guessing.
